message:
refactor(fj-lax): replace unreachable!() in concat scalar fast-path with Option collect

The concat primitive had a two-pass pattern: first an `all_scalar` check via
`iter().all(matches!(...))`, then a second pass that mapped scalar extraction
with an `unreachable!()` in the non-scalar arm. That's an unnecessary double
traversal plus a panic path that the type system could not verify, even though
the first check theoretically made it dead.

Collapse both passes into a single `collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()` over a map
that returns `Some(*lit)` for `Value::Scalar` and `None` otherwise. If every
element is a scalar we get `Some(elements)` and take the stacking branch; if
any element is non-scalar we fall through to the general tensor concat path.
Same behavior, no panic branch, one pass instead of two.

Also add a regression test `test_scan_mixed_output_kinds_returns_error` which
verifies that `eval_scan_functional` surfaces `EvalError::InvalidTensor(
ValueError::MixedAxisStackKinds)` when the scan body emits a scalar on the
first step and a tensor on the second step. Previously this path could hit
stack-kind inconsistency deep inside tensor stacking; asserting it now bubbles
a structured error guards against regressions that would reintroduce a panic.

Required importing `ValueError` from `fj_core` in the test module.
